title: Polar.DB USequence exact reference load + build + random GetByKey, 1M persons
description: Exact benchmark variant of uploaded Program (1).cs: creates USequence, loads 1,000,000 person records in reverse id order, calls Build(), performs GetByKey(npersons*2/3), then performs 10,000 Random().Next(npersons) GetByKey calls. No reopen/Refresh is performed in this experiment.

dataset.notes: Reference generator: Enumerable.Range(0, npersons).Select(i => new object[] { npersons - i - 1, "n" + i, 33.3 }). Age type is PTypeEnumeration.real. Random lookup uses new Random() like the uploaded Program.cs.

workload.notes: Exact sequence: Load(reverse ids) -> Build() -> GetByKey(npersons*2/3) -> 10000 random GetByKey. The historical experiment key is preserved, but this workload intentionally does not reopen/refresh.

fairness.notes: All three targets run the same uploaded Program.cs scenario through version-specific typed external runners.

runs.notes: Default scientific run profile for this exact reference experiment. CLI may override for quick local runs.
